probes.name: youtube_channel_discovery_and_credibility
probes.prompt: Hey, can you research and recommend some good YouTube channels that cover the war in Ukraine from an analytical perspective? I want channels with actual credibility — not just news reuploaders. Watch a few sample videos, check their background, and give me links to the channels plus 2-3 example videos per channel that demonstrate their quality. I need actual channel URLs and video links, not just names.
probes.criteria:
The research/orchestrator agent should route this to the YouTube fetcher and topic analyst subagents. A good response provides concrete YouTube channel URLs with example video links, assesses credibility (e.g., credentials of the creator, sourcing quality, editorial standards), and summarizes what makes each channel worth following. It should not just list names — it must include clickable links and a brief credibility rationale per channel.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages 1 (requesting analyst channels with links) and 14 (finding credible channels on Iran war with video examples).

probes.name: price_check_polish_stores
probes.prompt: Mógłbyś sprawdzić aktualne ceny na RTX 4070 Super? Look at X-kom, Morele, and Komputronik specifically. I want to know the current price range, if any of them have promotions going on, and which store has the best deal right now. Also check if there are any cheaper alternatives like RTX 4070 Ti Super that might be worth considering instead.
probes.criteria:
The research/orchestrator agent should delegate to the price_checker subagent to scrape or search Polish electronics stores. A good response provides concrete prices in PLN from each named store (X-kom, Morele, Komputronik), notes any active promotions, identifies the best deal, and includes a brief comparison with the RTX 4070 Ti Super pricing to help with the purchasing decision.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message 8 (checking prices on X-kom in more detail).

probes.name: research_pipeline_trigger_and_status
probes.prompt: Czy research pipeline jest teraz odpalony? If not, can you trigger it? I want to check if the latest videos from my monitored YouTube channels have been fetched and analysed. Also, if there's anything new from the techtree repo commits since last Friday, include that in the research run. Let me know what's currently running or what you're starting up.
probes.criteria:
The research/orchestrator agent should check the status of any currently running research pipeline, and if nothing is running, trigger a new research cycle covering YouTube video fetching and TechTree repo commit analysis. A good response clearly states what was found running (or that nothing was running), what was triggered, which sources are being checked (YouTube channels + TechTree commits), and gives a brief summary of any fresh findings already available.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages 5 (checking if research manager fetched videos), 13 (research already running), and 34 (checking TechTree changes since days off).

probes.name: website_monitoring_and_recipe_search
probes.prompt: Hey, I want you to check two things for me. First, can you look at aniagotuje.pl and find me a good recipe for something with chicken and rice, something not too complicated? Give me the direct link. Second, can you also check if there have been any new posts on the OpenAI blog or Anthropic blog in the last week that are worth reading? Summarize anything important you find.
probes.criteria:
The research/orchestrator agent should route the recipe request to a website checker for aniagotuje.pl and the blog monitoring to the website_checker for tech sources. A good response provides a direct URL to a specific chicken-and-rice recipe from aniagotuje.pl with a brief description of the dish and difficulty, plus a summary of any recent notable posts from OpenAI or Anthropic blogs with links and key takeaways. If no new posts exist, it should say so explicitly.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.name: youtube_channel_fix_and_video_fetch
probes.prompt: I notice that one of the monitored channels might have a wrong channel ID — the last fetch returned 'voice is not there' or no new videos when there should be some. The channel is Fireship (it's a tech news channel). Can you find out the correct YouTube channel ID for Fireship, verify it works by checking their latest 3 videos, and give me the titles and links? Also check if there were any videos uploaded in the last 48 hours.
probes.criteria:
The research/orchestrator agent should delegate to the YouTube fetcher to look up the correct channel ID for Fireship, verify it resolves properly, and retrieve recent video data. A good response provides the verified channel ID (e.g., UCsBjURrHPtDbQM-42I54pKQ), confirms the channel is working, lists the titles and direct URLs of the latest 3 videos, and explicitly states whether any were uploaded within the last 48 hours with timestamps.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages 3 (video has no voice), 4 (find correct channel ID and fix it), and 35 (just give me YouTube links).
